title: Ouratea cearensis
taxonomy: Ouratea cearensis is a member of the family Ochnaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the Ochna family. This family is part of the order Ochnales, which is a distinct order within the clade Rosanae. The class and phylum of Ouratea cearensis are more broadly classified as Magnoliopsida and Angiosperms, respectively. Ouratea cearensis is also a part of the kingdom Plantae, encompassing all types of plants. The domain of Ouratea cearensis is Eukarya, a group of organisms characterized by the presence of a true nucleus.

intro: Ouratea cearensis is a plant species commonly known as "jurema preta," belonging to the family Ochnaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to help alleviate symptoms of diabetes, hypertension, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ouratea cearensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, such as isoorientin, quercetin, and 1-O-β-D-glucopyranosyl-isorhapontigenin. In traditional herbal medicine, Ouratea cearensis is often prepared as a tea or infused in water, and it is also used to make herbal extracts and tinctures.
